name: Charlemagne
description: Charlemagne, also known as Charles the Great, was King of the Franks from 768, King of the Lombards from 774, and Emperor of the Romans from 800. He united much of Europe during the early Middle Ages and laid the foundations for modern France and Germany. Charlemagne's reign was marked by military campaigns, religious reform, and a cultural renaissance. He is revered for his strength, piety, and dedication to education and justice. The legends surrounding Charlemagne depict him as a wise and just ruler, a valiant warrior, and a devout Christian. He figures prominently in the Matter of France, a cycle of heroic poems and romances. His legacy includes the establishment of a strong centralized government, the promotion of literacy and learning, and the spread of Christianity throughout Europe. He is often portrayed as a symbol of European unity and a defender of the faith. His impact on the political, social, and cultural landscape of Europe is undeniable, solidifying his place as a pivotal figure in history and legend. He is also known for his fierce temper when angered, and his great love of his family.
subtitle: King, Emperor, and Legendary Hero
achievements: Unification of much of Western Europe

keyMyths.title: Charlemagne's Conquests
keyMyths.description: Charlemagne led numerous military campaigns, expanding his empire and spreading Christianity. His battles against the Saxons were particularly long and bloody, resulting in their forced conversion.
keyMyths.source: The History of the Franks by Gregory of Tours

keyMyths.title: The Coronation as Emperor
keyMyths.description: In 800 AD, Pope Leo III crowned Charlemagne Emperor of the Romans in Rome. This event marked the revival of the Roman Empire in the West and solidified Charlemagne's power.

keyMyths.title: The Song of Roland
keyMyths.description: This epic poem tells the story of Roland, Charlemagne's brave knight, who dies in battle against the Basques. The poem highlights themes of loyalty, courage, and Christian faith.

sources.author: Einhard
sources.work: Vita Karoli Magni (The Life of Charlemagne)
sources.description: A biography of Charlemagne written by a member of his court.
sources.text: A biography of Charlemagne written by a member of his court.

sources.author: Gregory of Tours
sources.work: Historia Francorum (The History of the Franks)
sources.description: A historical account of the Frankish kingdom.
sources.text: A historical account of the Frankish kingdom.
